era_context: Mid-Bronze Age, with Egypt under Thutmose III at the height of imperial power. The Hittite Empire was consolidating its position in Anatolia. Kassite Babylon ruled Mesopotamia. The Mycenaean civilization was developing in the Aegean. The Shang Dynasty ruled in China. This was an era of monumental architecture, long-distance trade, and interstate diplomacy.

events.title: Thutmose III's reign; peak of Egyptian imperial power
events.description: Thutmose III remained pharaoh of Egypt, presiding over the empire at its territorial and administrative zenith. His military campaigns had secured Egyptian dominance from Nubia to the Levant. Temple construction, particularly at Karnak and Luxor, continued at a high level. The pharaoh's authority and the efficiency of the Egyptian bureaucratic system were unmatched in the ancient world.

sources.name: Karnak Temple inscriptions and reliefs; Egyptian administrative records

events.certainty_note: Thutmose III's reign is well-documented in Egyptian sources. The specific year is assigned by convention within his reign period (c. 1479–1425 BCE).

disconfirming_evidence: No specific disconfirming evidence for this year. The broader chronological uncertainties of the Bronze Age apply (see earlier entries).
historiographic_note: The historical record for 1536 BCE is sparse and relies almost entirely on Egyptian temple inscriptions and administrative records. No contemporary written records from other regions are available for this specific year. The narrative is reconstructed from broader patterns of Egyptian imperial administration and monumental construction. The year-by-year history of this period is largely a matter of convention rather than specific documented events.

graph_edges.note: Continuation of Thutmose III's reign and Egyptian imperial consolidation.
